reasoning: Base value is 3.85 on Aug 29. Historically 3.85 is an isolated minimum of a recent downtrend, going from 4.14 in May, 4.09 in July, down to 3.85. The FRED stat engine gives a 68% chance of an increase relative to 3.85 over a week due to mean reversion and volatility. We are looking at a series whose mean seems to be around 4.10 earlier this year but trend is downward. The Fed Funds Rate is 4.25%-4.50%. A 50 bps cut is expected in the rest of 2025 according to Morningstar, which would take to 3.75%-4.00% by end 2025. Given 1-yr yields are heavily anticipating Fed cuts, a decrease to 3.85 implies the market is already pricing in a decent amount of cuts. Since 1-yr treasury yield maps strongly onto expected average Fed Funds rate over the next year (which would cover late 2025-late 2026), and Morningstar expects 0.75 points cut in 2026, the 1-year Treasury Yield might trend down longer term to track this (to ~3.5%-3.25%). Thus, the longer the horizon, the more likely the 1-year yield will fall definitively below 3.85. For Sept 7, it's very soon, market volatility should give ~65% chance of being above the unusually low 3.85 print on Aug 29. By Sept 30, a Fed meeting might happen (typically Sept), bringing a rate cut that locks in the lower yield, lowering prob to ~58%. By Nov 29, further cuts and economic settling might push yields lower, prob ~52%. By Feb 2026, with 2026 rate cuts coming into clear view, prob of being >3.85 is slightly bellow 50%, roughly 48%.
